title: Big Gigs for phones = Quantum Leap for Society! (fm Audiobooks)

enrichment.summary: Kyle argues that loading all of history's copyright-free audiobooks and ebooks onto cell phones represents a civilizational turning point, enabling instant fact-checking with primary sources during conversations. He describes his own collection of 1600 audiobooks from LibriVox and Kindle, makes a case that Jeff Bezos/Amazon is the main obstacle by keeping Kindle files in a proprietary format, and predicts audio books will eventually displace music as the dominant phone media.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Having all historical audiobooks on your phone is the moment of societal enlightenment and civilizational uplift
Amazon is greedily blocking the conversion of copyright-free Kindle books to MP3, and Jeff Bezos should remove that barrier
Ancient primary sources are more authoritative than Wikipedia because no one can dispute what was written 2000 years ago
Audiobooks will eventually displace music; music will become background jingles once people discover historical audio
The Roman Lupercalia was just a full-moon naked running party, not devil worship — ancient feasts were celebrations, not satanic rituals
